title: Bhu Varaha Swamy temple
tagline: Bhu Varaha Swamy temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Bhu Varaha Swamy temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Bhu Varaha Swamy temple is a Hindu temple, located at Srimushnam, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: The Bhu Varaha Swamy temple is a Hindu temple, located at Srimushnam, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the South Indian style of architecture, the temple is dedicated to Varaha (Bhu Varaha Swamy), the boar-avatar of the god Vishnu and his consort Lakshmi as Ambujavalli Thayar. It is Classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition. One of the greatest Hindu scholars of Vaishnava Tattvavada (Dvaita) philosophy, Madhvacharya, have come and resided in this temple many times.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhu Varaha Swamy temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhu Varaha Swamy temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhu Varaha Swamy temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (104 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (103 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhu Varaha Swamy temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhu Varaha Swamy temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhu Varaha Swamy temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bhu Varaha Swamy temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhu Varaha Swamy temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Srimushnam, Sriraman, Ariyalur, Varadarajanpettai, Alapakkam, Cuddalore, Authukurichi.
